title: HTTP Representations, Validators, Ranges, and File Transfer · Go Semantics Case: Byte Range Semantics
summary: A download endpoint guesses MIME types, buffers large bodies, ignores validators and Range, and treats Content-Disposition text as trusted HTML. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the byte range semantics increment in HTTP file-representation and transfer contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse and evaluate single byte ranges, suffix ranges, satisfiable bounds, 206, Content-Range, Accept-Ranges, and 416.
Apply ETag and Last-Modified validators with If-Match, If-None-Match, If-Range, 304, 412, and changed-state evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Byte Range Semantics · Theory Byte Range Semantics
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 4110 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate conditional inclusive byte ranges and response-body cleanup; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Parse and evaluate single byte ranges, suffix ranges, satisfiable bounds, 206, Content-Range, Accept-Ranges, and 416.
